{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "豊受大神宮}}" to "豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

"Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq-Mike Shrine (Ise)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 3]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|みけしんしや}} [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]
