13ページ。 地域住民からは[[産土神]]として信仰され、「辰神さん」と呼ばれている伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：47ページ。 == 概要 == 伊勢の海の玄関口として栄えた[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[神社港]]（かみやしろこう）に鎮座する伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：46 - 47ページ。[[境内]]は[[池]]と[[堀]]で囲まれている学研パブリッシング（2013）：63ページ。この堀は、海水の出入りした[[入り江]]の名残とされる阜嵐健"[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html 御食神社社]"（2013年9月29日閲覧。）。 [[祭神]]は、水戸御饗都神（みなとのみけつかみ）。神社港で揚がった[[海産物]]を伊勢神宮に調進する[[神饌|御饌]]の神である。「水戸」は海水の入り込む「港」を意味する。 社殿は一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれている式内社研究会 編（1990）：258ページ。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。本殿に向かい合う位置に[[舟]]の形をした[[手水鉢]]が置かれている。「辰の井」と呼ばれる[[井戸]]が境内にあり、火除の神として崇敬を集めている伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：46 - 47ページ。また、神社港の[[御木曳|お木曳き]]の奉曳団は「辰組」を名乗る{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=コース03 船参宮ルート 〜和船「みずき」を使って外宮・内宮参拝〜| publisher=伊勢市観光協会 |accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}}。 社名は、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』などでは「御食神社」で「ミケノ」という[[ルビ|傍訓]]を付している式内社研究会 編（1990）：255ページ。このほか、『齋宮式』では「御饗社」と記し、「ミアルシ」の傍訓を付す。白山芳太郎は「ミアルシ」は「ミアヘツ」の誤記であるとし、「ミアヘツ」が古い御食神社の呼称であり、「御食」・「御饗」と2つの表記が行われていたと述べている式内社研究会 編（1990）：255 - 257ページ。 == 歴史 == 『倭姫命世記』に記された「水饗神社」（みけじんじゃ）が起源であるとされる。水饗神社は、鷲取老翁（わしとりのおきな）が[[倭姫命]]に清水を奉った功績を讃えて創建されたもので、その時に清水を汲んだと伝わる「辰の井」が境内にある。伊勢神宮の摂社の定義より『延喜式神名帳』成立、すなわち[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前に創建されたのは確実である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：22ページ。また、『止由気宮儀式帳』にも記載があることから[[延暦]]23年（[[804年]]）以前から存在したことになる。『神名秘書』では、鎮座地を「箕曲郷大口村」とする式内社研究会 編（1990）：257ページ。[[櫻井勝之進]]は、御食神社に関連して、以下のように述べている櫻井（1970）：93ページ。 {{Quotation|勢田川下流の「水戸の御食都神の社」（『儀式帳』）を含むことは、この河口港が早くから要衝であったことを示すものであろうか。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』93ページ}} [[中世]]には[[祭祀]]が断絶し、[[寛文]]3年（[[1663年]]）に現社地で再興された。[[明治4年]]（[[1871年]]）、[[近代社格制度]]の創設により、外宮摂社でありながら、[[度会県]]管轄の村社に列し、伊勢神宮と度会県から二重に所管されることとなった櫻井（1991）：271ページ。こうした二重管轄はほかの神宮摂末社でも発生したため、神宮司庁は[[教部省]]に諮ったところ、11の末社が神宮の管轄から外れた一方、[[皇大神宮]]（内宮）末社の[[加努弥神社]]と御食神社はそのまま二重管轄とすることが[[明治5年]]4月（グレゴリオ暦：1872年5月）に申し渡された。加努弥神社では[[1881年]]（明治14年）12月に[[氏子]]が内宮末社と村社の分離を求め、[[1886年]]（明治19年）に分離が決定するに至ったが、御食神社に関しては分離が提起されたという記録はなく、分離問題はなかったと見られる櫻井（1991）：276 - 278ページ。 == 祭事 == [[File:Well of Tatsu in Ise.jpg|thumb|辰の井]] 御食神社では、新年最初の[[辰]]の日に「お水取り」が行われる{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=御食神社初辰の日の「お水取り」 伊勢市のイベント情報|work=観光三重| publisher=三重県観光連盟 |date=2013-01-02| accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}}。「お水取り」では、境内にある「辰の井」から汲み上げた水（初水）を地元住民が参拝者に配る。この水で清めると1年間[[水難]]・[[火災|火難]]を避けることができるとの伝承から、各家庭では家の周囲に水をかけ、残り水を[[台所]]に供える習わしとなっている{{Cite web|和書| url=http://www.ise-kanko.jp/meguri/|title=お伊勢めぐり上級編| publisher=伊勢市観光協会|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1623-20/www.ise-kanko.jp/meguri/|archivedate=2013-09-29}}。[[2013年]]（平成25年）は、[[1月2日]]に行われた。 [[お白石持]]の際には、辰の井の水を道に撒きながら御白石を奉献する伊勢文化舎 編（2013）：7ページ。 == 交通 == 御食神社は、古い街並みの少し残る、神社港の街の中にある。 * [[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]「神社港」[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩約3分（約300[[メートル|m]]）。 * [[国道23号]]（[[南勢バイパス]]）「小木町1」[[交差点]]から北東方向へ進み、[[三重県道201号宇治山田港伊勢市停車場線]]経由、約2分（約1.4[[キロメートル|km]]）。付近に[[駐車場]]はない。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25年8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[宇治山田港]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:みけしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

revision.comment: Bot: cleanup/reformat
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=draft}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=draft}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=draft}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "伊勢国の式内社一覧|" to "伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine
Former village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise, Mie|qq=}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211721]] Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg|270px]] |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Toyouke Daijingu" to "Toyōke Daijingū"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211721]] Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|qid=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|qq=|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|qq=|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[sn:Mike Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]]
