It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
[[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211721]] Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|WD=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17211721]] Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|WD=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|WD=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)" to "Kokuhei-sha"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Mike Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|WD=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

"Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Mike Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Mike shrine, Ise 2.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, Jinja Port, Minami-Kōji 1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 30 |Latitude Seconds = 43.6 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 44 |Longitude Seconds = 12.7 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Mito Miketsu no Kami |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine
Former Village shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Tatsukami-san |Festivals = |Rituals = Omizutori, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} |location = {{ill|Kamiyashirokō|WD=Q17226485}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.455|N|136.725175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} [[File:Mike shrine, Ise.jpg|thumb|Shrine hall]] '''Mike Shrine''' (御食神社, *Mike-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is Ranked 15th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 11–13. Locally, it is revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace) and affectionately called "Tatsukami-san." Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 47. == Overview == Mikenojinja (御食神社) is located in {{ill|Kamiya-shiro Port|ja|神社港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiya-shiro Port|1=Kamiyashirokō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226485}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, a port that flourished as the maritime gateway to Ise.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The precincts are surrounded by a pond and moat, believed to be remnants of a former inlet connected to the sea.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.63Furukawa Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131213130254/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080123-01.html Mikenojinja Shrine]" (accessed September 29, 2013) The enshrined deity is {{ill|Minato no Miketsukami|ja|水戸御饗都神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato no Miketsukami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the god of sacred offerings (御饌) made from marine products landed at Kamiya-shiro Port for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. "Minato" refers to a port where seawater reaches inland. The shrine building is enclosed by a single tamagaki (sacred fence) and has no saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall stands a chozubachi (water basin) shaped like a boat. A well called "Tatsu no I" within the precincts is revered as a fire prevention talisman.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.46-47 The local festival group is known as the "Tatsu-gumi."{{Cite web|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|title=Course 03: Boat Pilgrimage Route|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1615-48/www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course03.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} == History == The shrine traces its origins to the "Miketsu Jinja" (水饗神社) mentioned in the *Yamato-hime-no-mikoto Seiki*. It was founded to honor the service of WASHITORI no Okina, who presented pure water to {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. The "Tatsu no I" well in the precincts is said to be the very source from which the water was drawn. Mikenojinja was certainly established before 927 CE (Enchō 5), as it appears in the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.22 It also appears in the *Toyukemiya Gishikichō*, suggesting it existed before 804 CE (Enryaku 23).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.258 In the early modern period, after a period of neglect, the shrine was restored at its current location in 1663 (Kanbun 3).Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.257 == Festivals == The "Water Drawing" ceremony (お水取り) is held annually on the first {{ill|Day of the Dragon|ja|辰の日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day of the Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the New Year.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|title=Annual Water Drawing Ceremony at Mikenojinja|publisher=Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation|accessdate=2013-09-29|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-0929-1017-39/www.kankomie.or.jp/event/detail_15247.html|archivedate=2013-09-29}} Water drawn from the "Tatsu no I" well is distributed to worshippers, believed to offer protection against fire and water disasters for the year. During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

During the {{ill|Oshiraishi-mochi|ja|お白石持|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshiraishi-mochi|1=Oshiraishimochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225061}} ritual, water from the well is sprinkled along the path as white stones are carried to the shrine.Ise Bunka Publishing (2013): p.7 == Access == Mikenojinja is located in the historic Kamiya-shiro Port district. * **By Bus:** Approx. 3 minutes on foot from the "Kamiya-shiro Port" bus stop served by Mie Kotsu. * **By Car:** About 2 minutes (1.4 km) from the "Ogicho 1" intersection on National Route 23 via Mie Prefectural Route 201. No dedicated parking is available. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『いせびとニュース第12号』残暑号、伊勢文化舎・伊勢市観光協会・おかげ参り推進委員会、平成25th year 8月17日、8p. * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[宇治山田港]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m04/0401.html 御食神社（みけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|version=100232694|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Mike Shrine (Ise) (Q17211721)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032}} || {{ill|Mike Shrine (Ise)|lt=Mike Shrine|ja|御食神社 (伊勢市)|WD=Q17211721}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || part of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|WD=Q11633343}} || — || {{coord|34.512024|136.736900|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181130 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17211721}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17211721]] [[ja:御食神社 (伊勢市)]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
